perturbation.original_text: In it's business, COMPANY has certain valuable technical and non-technical information, processes, samples, sources and supplies, including but not limited to certain training, literature, information, promotional plans and direction used in connection with COMPANY'S products which are VITAL to its business and success ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL") and, to guard the legitimate interests of the COMPANY, it is necessary for the COMPANY to protect the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by holding it confidential as TRADE SECRETS. After execution thereof, the COMPANY will disclose to RECIPIENT certain of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and RECIPIENT, through his/her activities and by virtue of this relationship with the COMPANY, will become acquainted with certain CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.changed_text: In it's business, COMPANY has certain valuable technical and non-technical information, processes, samples, sources and supplies used in connection with COMPANY'S products which are VITAL to its business and success ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). After execution thereof, the COMPANY will disclose to RECIPIENT certain of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and RECIPIENT, through his/her activities and by virtue of this relationship with the COMPANY, will become acquainted with certain CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'including but not limited to certain training, literature, information, promotional plans and direction' from the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' creates an in-text contradiction. Later sections discuss the recipient's access to 'special testing and training procedures and processes'. Since 'training' is removed from the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL', it becomes ambiguous whether the restrictions and protections around 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' apply to the training the recipient receives. This creates uncertainty in enforcement regarding the use and disclosure of the COMPANY's training materials by the recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In it's business, COMPANY has certain valuable technical and non-technical information, processes, samples, sources and supplies used in connection with COMPANY'S products which are VITAL to its business and success ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). After execution thereof, the COMPANY will disclose to RECIPIENT certain of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and RECIPIENT, through his/her activities and by virtue of this relationship with the COMPANY, will become acquainted with certain CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'including but not limited to certain training, literature, information, promotional plans and direction' from the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL'. The explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction because later sections discuss the recipient's access to 'special testing and training procedures and processes'. By removing 'training' from the definition, it becomes ambiguous whether the confidentiality restrictions apply to the training the recipient receives, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This constitutes a clear contradiction as the scope of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' is narrowed, potentially excluding 'training' which is discussed elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'special testing and training procedures and processes' from the list of items considered 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' in this section introduces an in-text contradiction. The initial definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' still implies broad protection, but this section specifically excludes those items, creating ambiguity about their legal status under the agreement. This contradicts the general confidentiality obligations and the trade secret designation, making it uncertain whether these processes are protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'special testing and training procedures and processes' within the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL'. The changed text omits these items from the definition, while the contradicted text (which is identical to the original) still includes these items. This creates a direct contradiction because one section includes them as confidential while the other does not, leading to uncertainty about their protected status under the contract. This is a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.changed_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason' the contract becomes internally contradictory. While it still prohibits reverse engineering, the omission creates ambiguity about whether the recipient can share CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL with third parties. This is a significant contradiction if other sections assume non-transferability as a core protection of the confidential information, and leaves a loophole that could be exploited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the transfer of confidential material to other companies or persons. The changed text removes this prohibition, creating a loophole. The 'Contradicted Text' (which is the same as the original text) inherently relies on the assumption that RECIPIENT cannot transfer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason. The omission directly conflicts with the expectation of confidentiality outlined in the original clause and, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, creates a contradiction by opening the possibility of sharing confidential material with third parties, undermining the intended protection.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.' creates an in-text contradiction. This sentence defined a specific obligation for the recipient to keep a log of individuals with access to confidential information. With this sentence gone, the remaining text vaguely states that the recipient has to keep this confidential but does not specify to keep a log of individuals with access to the confidential information. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear what safeguards the recipient must take with out this specific definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a specific requirement for the RECIPIENT to maintain a record or log of individuals with access to confidential information. The changed text removes this specific requirement, while the contradicted text (which is identical to the first part of the original text) states that the RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS. The omission of the logging requirement creates a contradiction because it is now unclear what 'adequate SAFEGUARDS' entail, potentially conflicting with an expectation (implied by the original text) that such logs should be maintained. This is an 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' because the removal of the sentence creates a direct conflict regarding the specific steps of maintaining safeguards.
